title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. diversifolia
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. diversifolia, also known as the grey-leaved or diversifolia santolina, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing digestive problems. The herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing the dried leaves with a solvent to create a medicinal extract.
